company.name: Brigade Electronics
company.description: Global manufacturer of vehicle safety systems for commercial vehicles and machinery — cameras, sensors, radar, AI vision, and white-sound reversing alarms

primary.name: Brigade Red
primary.usage: Signature wordmark colour, headers, CTAs, safety accents, call-to-action highlights

secondary.name: Charcoal
secondary.usage: Secondary text, UI elements, backgrounds — provides the professional, grounded feel

accent.name: Brigade Red (deep)
accent.usage: Safety-stripe dividers, angled red accents, danger indicators, important statistics

dark.name: Charcoal
dark.usage: Body text, secondary headings, dark backgrounds

white.name: White
white.usage: Primary background and negative space — ensures the red accents and technical imagery stand out

heading.usage: H1, H2, H3 headlines, wordmark — geometric sans-serif chosen for clarity in digital and print

body.usage: Body text, technical documentation, website copy — high legibility for safety specifications

mono.usage: Product codes, model numbers, technical specifications

brand_voice.tone: Plain-spoken expert — talks to operators, not boardrooms
brand_voice.style: Outcome-focused: lives saved, accidents prevented, compliance achieved

key_messages: Eliminate vehicle blind spots and prevent collisions
Patented bbs-tek white-sound — locatable, less noise pollution
AI vision detection that distinguishes humans from objects
Compliant with FORS, CLOCS, DVS Progressive Safe System
Trusted globally by transport, construction, and emergency services
value_propositions: Provide indisputable video evidence for incidents
Engineered for harsh environments — built to last

full.usage: Primary logo for documents, websites, vehicle decals

mark.usage: Icon/mark only for product labels, social media, favicons

imagery.style: Real working environments — HGVs, plant, refuse trucks, depots
imagery.photography: Drivers and operators in-situ, equipment installed on real vehicles, on-the-job action shots
imagery.illustrations: Technical diagrams of camera angles, blind-spot zones, sound directionality

visual_motif.name: Safety Stripe
visual_motif.description: Diagonal red accents and angled red bars used as graphical dividers and call-to-action highlights, subtly referencing the hazard markings found on commercial vehicles and construction sites
visual_motif.usage: Page headers, section breaks, CTA buttons, slide accents

linkedin.background: solid #FFFFFF with a diagonal #ED1C24 safety-stripe accent

twitter.background: #FFFFFF with #ED1C24 wordmark and charcoal body copy

instagram.background: high-contrast white/charcoal with red Safety Stripe diagonals
instagram.style: Clean, geometric, safety-focused — red accents over white negative space

powerpoint.accent_bar: #ED1C24 (left edge, full height — Safety Stripe motif)

do: Use Brigade Red (#ED1C24) as the signature colour, paired with Charcoal (#2B2B2B) and White
Apply the Safety Stripe motif — diagonal red accents as graphical dividers or CTA highlights, echoing hazard markings on commercial vehicles
Use Montserrat Bold for headlines and the wordmark; Open Sans or Inter for body copy
Lead with concrete safety outcomes (lives saved, accidents prevented)
Cite compliance standards by name (FORS, CLOCS, DVS, BS EN 7010)
Show real vehicles and operators, not stock office photography
Use plain, direct language — no jargon, no marketing fluff
Reference actual product codes (Backeye 360, bbs-tek, Backsense) where appropriate
dont: Don't reintroduce safety yellow — the 2024 rebrand moved to red/charcoal/white
Don't use pastel or muted colour palettes — high contrast is the brand
Don't use boardroom/financial imagery — Brigade speaks to operations
Don't make claims without evidence — link to test data or case studies
Don't undersell the safety stakes — this product prevents fatalities
Don't use Finabeo-style governance/transformation language — it's not the audience
Don't substitute fonts other than Montserrat / Open Sans / Inter without approval

metadata.notes: Updated 2026-04-16 to reflect Brigade Electronics' 2024 rebrand: signature Brigade Red (#ED1C24 / #D2232A deep), Charcoal (#2B2B2B), and White palette; Montserrat Bold wordmark/headings with Open Sans or Inter body copy; and the 'Safety Stripe' motif (diagonal red accents referencing hazard markings). Earlier 0.1 draft used an inferred yellow/black palette which has been removed.
next_steps: Confirm exact Pantone for Brigade Red with Brigade brand team
Provide official logo SVGs in multiple variants
Confirm font licensing for Montserrat in commercial materials
Validate compliance terminology and product names with product team
